questions.question: Which property of a light wave determines the color we perceive?
questions.explanation: Wavelength determines the color of light we perceive. Shorter wavelengths are seen as blue/violet, while longer wavelengths are seen as red/orange. Amplitude, by contrast, determines brightness.

questions.question: What does the amplitude of a sound wave determine about our perception of sound?

options.text: The timbre or tonal quality of the sound
The direction the sound is coming from

questions.explanation: The amplitude (height) of a sound wave determines its perceived loudness. Greater amplitude produces louder sounds. Pitch, by contrast, is determined by frequency (or wavelength).
